methods.name: ExecuteProtectedCode
methods.summary: Any synchronous method in the IRhinoAccountsManager class must be executed within the function passed to this method, or an InvalidOperationException will be thrown.
methods.remarks: The code within the function passed must be kept as breif as possible for performance and security reasons.

params.name: protectedCode
params.doc: A function returning an awaitable task that has a SecretKey passed to it. You will need to pass this secret key to any method you wish to call within IRhinoAccountsManager.

throws.when: Another operation is already in progress.

methods.name: ExecuteProtectedCodeAsync
methods.signature: public static Task ExecuteProtectedCodeAsync(Func<SecretKey, Task> protectedCode)
methods.summary: Any asynchronous method in the IRhinoAccountsManager class must be executed within the function passed to this method, or an InvalidOperationException will be thrown.
methods.remarks: The code within the function passed must be kept as breif as possible for performance and security reasons.

params.name: protectedCode
params.doc: A function returning an awaitable task that has a SecretKey passed to it. You will need to pass this secret key to any method you wish to call within IRhinoAccountsManager.

throws.when: Another operation is already in progress.

methods.name: GetAuthTokensAsync(String, String, SecretKey, CancellationToken)
methods.signature: public static Task<Tuple<IOpenIDConnectToken, IOAuth2Token>> GetAuthTokensAsync(string clientId, string clientSecret, SecretKey secretKey, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
methods.summary: Asynchronously retrieves auth tokens with the given criteria from the Rhino Accounts server.
methods.remarks: This method includes a default scope of 'openid', 'email', 'profile', and 'groups'. It should be suffifient for most workflows. This method shows the user a UI showing them the progress of the operation, and allows them to cancel it at any time. It also stores the retrieved tokens in a secure cache so they can be retrieved in the future by calling TryGetAuthTokens(String, SecretKey)

params.name: clientId
params.doc: The unique id of the client registered in Rhino Accounts.

params.name: clientSecret
params.doc: The secret of the client registered in Rhino Accounts

params.name: secretKey
params.doc: A special key that was handed to you in ExecuteProtectedCodeAsync(Func<SecretKey, Task>)

params.name: cancellationToken
params.doc: A token that can be used to signal that the operation should be cancelled.

throws.when: An invalid secretKey was passed, or the assembly is not allowed to call methods from IRhinoAccountsManager
An error occurred during the authentication process. You may look at the specific subclass of the exception gain more insight as to why the operation failed.
The operation was cancelled, either by internal decisions or because the cancellationToken was cancelled.

methods.name: GetAuthTokensAsync(String, String, IEnumerable<String>, String, Nullable<Int32>, Boolean, IProgress<RhinoAccoountsProgressInfo>, SecretKey, CancellationToken)
methods.signature: public static Task<Tuple<IOpenIDConnectToken, IOAuth2Token>> GetAuthTokensAsync(string clientId, string clientSecret, IEnumerable<string> scope, string prompt, Nullable<int> maxAge, bool showUI, IProgress<RhinoAccoountsProgressInfo> progress, SecretKey secretKey, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
methods.summary: Asynchronously retrieves auth tokens with the given criteria from the Rhino Accounts server.

params.name: clientId
params.doc: The unique id of the client registered in Rhino Accounts.

params.name: clientSecret
params.doc: The secret of the client registered in Rhino Accounts

params.name: scope
params.doc: The scope desired for the tokens. Valid scope values can be found in the Rhino Accounts documentation.

params.name: prompt
params.doc: The prompt of the request. See Rhino Accounts documentation for details. You may pass null if no prompt is desired.

params.name: maxAge
params.doc: The maxAge of the request. See Rhino Accounts documentation for details. You may pass null if no maxAge should be enforced.

params.name: showUI
params.doc: true if the user should see a UI showing the progress of the operation and a way to cancel it, or false if the UI should not be displayed. If false, it is strongly recommended that you pass a progress object and display your own UI to the user.

params.name: progress
params.doc: An object that will report the progress of the operation to the caller. If no progress is needed, you may pass null.

params.name: secretKey
params.doc: A special key that was handed to you in ExecuteProtectedCodeAsync(Func<SecretKey, Task>)

params.name: cancellationToken
params.doc: A token that can be used to signal that the operation should be cancelled.

throws.when: An invalid secretKey was passed, or the assembly is not allowed to call methods from IRhinoAccountsManager
An error occurred during the authentication process. You may look at the specific subclass of the exception gain more insight as to why the operation failed.
The operation was cancelled, either by internal decisions or because the cancellationToken was cancelled.

methods.name: RevokeAuthTokenAsync
methods.signature: public static Task RevokeAuthTokenAsync(IOAuth2Token oauth2Token, SecretKey secretKey, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
methods.summary: Invalidates/revokes an IOAuth2Token object from the Rhino Accounts server.
methods.remarks: If the OAuth 2 token is stored in the local cache, it will be removed from the cache as well.

params.name: oauth2Token
params.doc: The token to revoke.

params.name: secretKey
params.doc: A special key that was handed to you in ExecuteProtectedCodeAsync(Func<SecretKey, Task>)

params.name: cancellationToken
params.doc: A token that can be used to signal that the operation should be cancelled.

throws.when: An invalid secretKey was passed, or the assembly is not allowed to call methods from IRhinoAccountsManager
The operation was cancelled, either by internal decisions or because the cancellationToken was cancelled.

methods.name: TryGetAuthTokens(String, SecretKey)
methods.signature: public static Tuple<IOpenIDConnectToken, IOAuth2Token> TryGetAuthTokens(string clientId, SecretKey secretKey)
methods.summary: Attempts to return cached auth tokens that match the given criteria if any have been stored in cache.

params.name: clientId
params.doc: The unique id of the client registered in Rhino Accounts.

params.name: secretKey
params.doc: A special key that was handed to you in ExecuteProtectedCodeAsync(Func<SecretKey, Task>)

returns.doc: Cached tokens matching the exact criteria passed, or null if none can be found matching the criteria.

throws.when: An invalid secretKey was passed, or the assembly is not allowed to call methods from IRhinoAccountsManager

methods.name: TryGetAuthTokens(String, IEnumerable<String>, SecretKey)
methods.signature: public static Tuple<IOpenIDConnectToken, IOAuth2Token> TryGetAuthTokens(string clientId, IEnumerable<string> scope, SecretKey secretKey)
methods.summary: Attempts to return cached auth tokens that match the given criteria if any have been stored in cache.

params.name: clientId
params.doc: The unique id of the client registered in Rhino Accounts.

params.name: scope
params.doc: The scope desired for the tokens. Valid scope values can be found in the Rhino Accounts documentation.

params.name: secretKey
params.doc: A special key that was handed to you in ExecuteProtectedCodeAsync(Func<SecretKey, Task>)

returns.doc: Cached tokens matching the exact criteria passed, or null if none can be found matching the criteria.

throws.when: An invalid secretKey was passed, or the assembly is not allowed to call methods from IRhinoAccountsManager

methods.name: UpdateOpenIDConnectTokenAsync
methods.signature: public static Task<IOpenIDConnectToken> UpdateOpenIDConnectTokenAsync(IOpenIDConnectToken currentToken, IOAuth2Token oauth2Token, SecretKey secretKey, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
methods.summary: Updates an OpenID Connect token so that it contains the latest user information by contacting the Rhino Account's server userinfo endpoint using a compatible O
methods.remarks: If the OpenID Connect token is currently stored in the local cache, it will be updated to reflect the new token.

params.name: currentToken
params.doc: The existing OpenID Connect token that you wish to updated with the latest user information.

params.name: oauth2Token
params.doc: A valid OAuth2 token used for authorization. The OAuth2 token must have been issued together with the OpenID Connect token passed or a RhinoAccountsAuthTokenMismatchException will be thrown.

params.name: secretKey
params.doc: A special key that was handed to you in ExecuteProtectedCodeAsync(Func<SecretKey, Task>)

params.name: cancellationToken
params.doc: A token that can be used to signal that the operation should be cancelled.

returns.doc: The updated OpenIDConnectToken based on the original token passed to this method.

throws.when: An invalid secretKey was passed, or the assembly is not allowed to call methods from IRhinoAccountsManager
An error occurred during the authentication process. You may look at the specific subclass of the exception gain more insight as to why the operation failed.
The operation was cancelled, either by internal decisions or because the cancellationToken was cancelled.
